name: Helicone API
what_it_does: Helicone is an LLM observability and proxy platform. Drop one header into any OpenAI (or Anthropic, Azure, Mistral, etc.) API call and all requests are logged, analyzed, and monitored. Features include: request/response logging, cost tracking, latency monitoring, user-level analytics, prompt management and versioning, A/B testing, smart caching (LLM semantic cache), rate limiting, and custom dashboards. Supports agent tracing with session IDs and custom properties for debugging multi-step agent workflows.
use_cases: Monitoring LLM API costs across all providers from a single dashboard
Debugging failed agent runs by replaying exact prompts and responses
Tracking LLM latency and error rates in production agent systems
Implementing semantic caching to reduce duplicate LLM API costs
User-level usage tracking and quota enforcement in multi-tenant AI apps
Prompt versioning and A/B testing to measure prompt quality improvements
Session-based tracing of multi-step agent workflows for debugging
not_for: Teams that cannot route LLM traffic through a third-party proxy for security/compliance reasons
Non-LLM API monitoring (use Datadog or similar for general API monitoring)
best_when: You're running LLM-powered agents in production and need visibility into costs, errors, and performance. The proxy-based architecture means zero code changes beyond adding one header — just change your base URL and add the Helicone-Auth header.
avoid_when: You cannot accept a proxy in your LLM call path for latency or compliance reasons, or your compliance requirements prohibit routing API responses through third parties.

alternatives.reason: LangSmith focuses on LangChain-native tracing with richer agent workflow visualization; Helicone is provider-agnostic and works with any LLM API
W&B is better for ML experiment tracking; Helicone is better for production LLM API monitoring

auth.notes: Two keys required: (1) Helicone-Auth: Bearer {HELICONE_API_KEY} added to proxied LLM calls, (2) original LLM API key still passed as Authorization header. The SDK handles this automatically. Management API uses the same Helicone key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 10,000 requests/month free. No credit card required. Free tier includes logging, cost tracking, and basic dashboard.
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth: $20/month for 100,000 requests. Pro: $200/month for 2M requests. Enterprise: custom. Caching and advanced features available on paid tiers.
pricing.notes: Open source (MIT) — can self-host for unlimited logging without per-request costs. Managed cloud is the easiest option. Self-hosting requires Clickhouse and Postgres.

performance.rate_limits: Proxy adds ~5-20ms latency overhead per call. No rate limits on the proxy itself — limits are enforced by the underlying LLM API.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Proxy errors are clearly distinguished from upstream LLM errors. Dashboard surfaces error rates and message content. SDK wraps errors to indicate whether failure was in proxy or LLM.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Proxy is pass-through — idempotency depends on underlying LLM API. Session IDs and request IDs in headers enable deduplication in dashboard.
known_agent_gotchas: Two separate API keys (Helicone key + LLM key) must both be present — common misconfiguration point
Semantic cache hits return cached responses which may be stale for time-sensitive agent queries — disable caching for time-sensitive tools
Session ID headers must be consistently set across all agent steps or tracing will be fragmented
Self-hosted Helicone requires Clickhouse which has significant infrastructure overhead
Webhook notifications for specific events (errors, high cost) are on paid tiers only
Response streaming (SSE) is fully supported but session-level aggregation may be delayed
